samples.gvd:
module signal_generator
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    output reg [4:0] wave
);

reg state;

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (~rst_n) begin
        state <= 0;
        wave <= 0;
    end
    else begin
        case (state)
            0: begin
                if (wave == 31) begin
                    state <= 1;
                end
                else begin
                    wave <= wave + 1;
                end
            end
            1: begin
                if (wave == 0) begin
                    state <= 0;
                end
                else begin
                    wave <= wave - 1;
                end
            end
        endcase
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="signal_generator">
    <description>Triangle Wave signal generator module that generates a waveform by incrementing and decrementing a 5-bit signal named "wave". The waveform cycles between 0 and 31, which is incremented or decremented by 1.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <output id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">5-bit output waveform signal representing the generated waveform.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="state">State register used to control the state of the waveform generation.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="state">State register used to control the state of the waveform generation.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">5-bit output waveform signal representing the generated waveform.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The module includes a register, state, used to control the state of the waveform generation.
        The module includes a register, wave, with a width of 5 bits, which represents the generated waveform.
        The state and wave registers are updated in the always block, triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).
        On reset, indicated by ~rst_n, the state register is cleared to 0, and the wave register is cleared to 0.
        The waveform generation is controlled using a case statement based on the current state:
        If the state is 0, the waveform (wave) is incremented by 1.
        If the waveform reaches 31 (wave == 31), the state is transitioned to 1.
        If the state is 1, the waveform is decremented by 1.
        If the waveform reaches 0 (wave == 0), the state is transitioned back to 0.
        The waveform generation continues to cycle between 0 and 31 based on the state transitions.
    </implementation>
</module>
